description: .loc with scalar row key coerces mixed-dtype row to common dtype

_meta.explanation: df.loc[0] (scalar) returns a Series where all values are cast to a common dtype — the int column 'a' becomes float 1.0 because 'b' is float. df.loc[[0]] (list) returns a DataFrame, preserving each column's own dtype.
_meta.curriculum_note: Level 2. Scalar vs list .loc is one of the trickiest pandas type preservation issues.
_meta.generation_recipe: pandas_mc injection: .loc with scalar row key coerces mixed-dtype row to common dtype
